title: OUTREACH CENTER - 211info
text: CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 383-2793 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk-ins accepted. Recommended to call ahead. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: General services: None Gas vouchers: Driver's license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Crook, Deschutes, and Jefferson Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Case/Care Management * Homeless Veterans General Legal Aid * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Homeless Drop In Centers * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Street Outreach Programs Street Outreach Programs * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Telephone Facilities * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans Transportation Passes * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Veteran Employment Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
